items.title: Here's what you need to know about today's Gina Rinehart court ruling

children.content: Today's blockbuster court ruling over mining tenements worth billions of dollars in WA's iron ore rich Pilbara is the culmination of a complicated legal battle spanning more than a decade that has pitted family against family and siblings against each other.

items.title: Split decision as Gina Rinehart, Wright family both claim legal wins

children.content: Gina Rinehart is dealt a blow, with a court finding the family of her father's former business partner has the right to potentially hundreds of millions of dollars in royalties from lucrative Pilbara mining assets.

image.altText: A woman in a black jacket and pink shirt steps on stage

items.title: Dash cam video of near-misses on WA highways prompts road safety call

children.content: A 21-year-old woman was charged with reckless driving after dash cam video allegedly caught her overtaking a triple road train in an "unsafe" manner.

image.altText: A still image of a car almost colliding with a truck, captured by a dash cam.

items.title: Amid chronic nursing shortages in country WA, Hamish is showing what it's like

children.content: Content creator Hamish Briggs is sharing his journey working as a nurse in Western Australia's far north, as the healthcare sector grapples with workforce shortages in regional areas.

items.title: Perth man on trial for alleged rape, assault of several women

children.content: Benjamin Bruce, 26, appeared before the WA District Court on charges including sexual penetration without consent and assault against several women.

image.altText: A medium shot with a sign reading: District Court of Western Australia.

items.title: Perth GP suspended for helping patient use $18,500 in super on cosmetic lipo

children.content: The healthcare regulator and tax office are cracking down on health practitioners who help patients access their superannuation early after one Perth doctor signed off on a $20,000 withdrawal for liposuction.

image.altText: A doctor and nurse in an operating room in a hospital.

items.title: Four million litres of diesel secured by WA government 'won't touch the sides'

children.content: The diesel is set to arrive in Western Australia "in the coming weeks" as part of a strategic stockpile to address acute, localised fuel shortages across the state, but it has been dismissed as "woefully inadequate".

items.title: Cameron Green suffers IPL nightmare with bat and ball

children.content: Cameron Green has suffered another challenging outing in the Indian Premier League as he struggles to live up to his tag as the most expensive overseas player.

items.title: Travellers shocked by suspension of WA tourist flights

children.content: Rising fuel costs due to the war in Iran see Qantas suspend Jetstar flights between Sydney and Busselton.

image.altText: Passengers arrive at Busselton Airport after the flight from Sydney.

items.title: What a 'super El Niño' would mean for Australia's weather in 2026

children.content: Australia's weather is expected to shift rapidly thanks to the probable emergence of an incoming El Niño, meaning drought, extreme heat and bushfires are on the way.

articles.title: Drought fuels biggest cattle sell-off in 20 years, as El Niño looms

children.content: More than 500,000 head of cattle have already been sold across New South Wales this year, with Scone seeing its biggest sale in 20 years.

items.title: King Charles to have tea with Donald Trump during US trip

children.content: King Charles will have tea and a private meeting with US President Donald Trump during his US state visit later this month, with trips to Washington, New York and Virginia.

items.title: WA children to get needle-free flu vaccine

children.content: Western Australia is offering free needle-free flu vaccines for children in a bid to curb declining inoculation rates and prevent another deadly influenza season.

image.altText: A young boy receives a nasal spray flu vaccine

items.title: ASX follows Wall Street higher, oil prices sink on hope of further US-Iran peace talks — as it happened

children.content: The Australian share market has risen in morning trade as global investors bet on the US-Iran war being over within months, despite fuel shortages, higher inflation and a possible economic slowdown.

children.content: The Duke of Sussex is joined by Western Bulldogs players at a Movember event in Melbourne's west.

items.title: WA government eyes off unused COVID quarantine facility for prison

children.content: The WA government flags the possible conversion of the Bullsbrook COVID quarantine centre into a prison amid an "unprecedented" rise in the number of inmates being crammed into cells across the state.

children.content: Filmed in director Ian Tuason's childhood home and based on his own experiences caring for his dying parents, Undertone has become a sleeper hit.

items.title: Player's umpire abuse verdict raises questions about AFL's 'conflict of interest'

children.content: The reporting of Port Adelaide player Zak Butters for umpire abuse sparks debate about whether officials should be involved with betting and if better audio recordings should be available from games.

items.title: Collard hearing leaves no winners as queer community further bruised

children.content: Lance Collard is handed a monster suspension after being found guilty of using a homophobic slur in the first AFL disciplinary hearing.

image.altText: Lance Collard stands with both arms in the air during a St Kilda vs West Coast Eagles AFL match in 2025.

items.title: 'Potential is significant': The untapped fuel source already on our shores

children.content: Australia controls less than 20 per cent of our liquid fuel supply and relies on imports for the other 80-plus per cent we need to keep the nation moving. But proponents of biofuels argue it does not need to be this way.

image.altText: Yellow sign states "pump not in use" is attached to the petrol bowser.

items.title: Man who sent threatening letters was 'drinking himself to sleep'

children.content: A man who sent threatening letters to a Sydney mosque was "drinking himself to sleep" and "fell into some type of internet rabbit hole", a court hears.

image.altText: lakemba mosque

items.title: Teenager 'begging' minister to deport abuser who had visa reinstated

children.content: A girl who was sexually assaulted in Perth by a UK citizen when she was nine years old writes to Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke, pleading with him to cancel the man's visa after he avoided deportation on appeal.

gallery.altText: Tony Burke wears grey suit and is speaking at a press conference.

items.title: Massive hospital spend creates more questions than answers

children.content: The WA government is splashing an additional $1.5 billion on health, bringing the state's total hospital spend to $5.5 billion over the next four years. But where exactly will the extra money go?

image.altText: WA Premier Roger Cook wearing a white hard hat and yellow hi-ves vest speaks during a media conference at a construction site.

items.title: Reality star left fearing death after fake weight loss jab

children.content: Aisleyne Horgan-Wallace lay on her bed, wincing in pain, convinced she was about to die after injecting herself with a cheap, unregulated weight loss drug.

items.title: World-famous Horizontal Falls to allow more tourists, with cultural focus

children.content: Tourism operators, the state government and traditional owners strike an agreement about the future of tourism at the famed Horizontal Falls in WA's Kimberley.
